subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 1.1.1: Explain the nature of God seen through His attributes
indicator_description: Explain the nature of God through His attributes in the three major religions
examplars:
1. Identify the attributes of God. E.g. omnipotent, omnipresent, omniscient, love, patience. [cite: 8133]
2. Explain the attributes of God in English and in their local languages. E.g., Amowia - giver of sunlight (Akan); Binnamdanaa - the Creator of all creatures (Dagbani); Mawu Kitikata - God is the source of life (Ewe) [cite: 8133]
3. Write an essay on the attributes of God and present your works for class discussion. Learners are encouraged to make use of resources on the internet for more of the attributes of God from the three major religions. [cite: 8133]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 1.1.1: Explain the nature of God seen through His attributes
indicator_description: Describe ways in which you demonstrate attributes of God in your life
examplars:
1. In groups, ask learners to identify the attributes of God that are found in humankind and present to class for discussion E.g. love, patience, merciful. [cite: 8133]
2. Role play how they relate the attributes of God to their life. [cite: 8133]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 1.1.1: Explain the nature of God seen through His attributes
indicator_description: Identify the similarities in the way that the nature of God is understood through His attributes in the three major religions in Ghana
examplars: 1. Using Think-Pair-Share, learners identify the similarities in the way the nature of God is understood in His attributes in the three major religions. Examples: Everlasting (Tetekwaframoa, Alpha and Omega, Al-Awwalu wal A'khirun); Omnipotent (Mawu, Otumfour, El-Shaddai, Azza wa Jalla); Sustainer (Amosu, Amowia, Jehovah-Jireh, Al-Razak). [cite: 8139]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.1.1: Explain how worship is performed in the three major religions in Ghana and apply the moral lessons in the worship in their life.
indicator_description: Identify the types of worship in the three major religions
examplars:
1. Through questions and answers learners explain the meaning of worship. E.g. worship is the submission to the will of God or Allah. [cite: 8145]
2. Show pictures or video clip of people at worship. [cite: 8145]
3. Through Think-Pair Share, learners discuss the religion each worship belongs to. [cite: 8145]
4. Identify the types of worship in Christianity, Islam and Indigenous African Religions. E.g. private (individual or family) and congregational. [cite: 8145]
5. In three mixed ability groups, learners dramatise the types of worship in the three major religions. [cite: 8145]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.1.1: Explain how worship is performed in the three major religions in Ghana and apply the moral lessons in the worship in their life.
indicator_description: Describe the modes of worship in the three major religions
examplars: 1. In groups, learners to identify activities that take place during worship and report to class. Christianity (Meditation, Bible reading, etc.), Islam (Niyyat, ablution, etc.), Indigenous African Religion (libation, sacrifice, etc.). [cite: 8152]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.1.1: Explain how worship is performed in the three major religions in Ghana and apply the moral lessons in the worship in their life.
indicator_description: Identify and explain the moral lessons from worship
examplars:
1. Through questions and answers, learners identify the moral lessons from worship. E.g., It encourages discipline in society, togetherness and unity, etc. [cite: 8152]
2. Learners to dramatise how they relate the moral lessons from worship to their life. [cite: 8152]
3. Project Work: Consult a pastor/priest/an Imam and parents to discuss why we need to be in constant touch with God. [cite: 8152]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.1.1: Explain how worship is performed in the three major religions in Ghana and apply the moral lessons in the worship in their life.
indicator_description: Explain the significance of prayer
examplars:
1. Learners to explain the meaning of prayer. [cite: 8157]
2. In groups, learners to identify the types of prayer in the three major religions and present findings for discussion. Christianity (thanksgiving, etc.), Islam (Fard prayers, Ju'muah, etc.), Indigenous (thanksgiving, etc.). [cite: 8157]
3. Learners use skits to demonstrate how prayer is performed in the three major religions. [cite: 8157]
4. Learners should compare the use of prayer across the three major religions in Ghana and identify similarities and differences. [cite: 8160]
5. In groups, learners to discuss the significance of prayer in their daily life. [cite: 8160]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.2.1: Analyse and apply the moral values in religious songs and recitations
indicator_description: Differentiate between religious songs and non-religious songs.
examplars:
1. Explain the differences between religious songs and non-religious songs. [cite: 8167]
2. State the characteristic of religious songs. Learners listen to a variety of songs and identify if they are religious songs or not. [cite: 8167]
3. In groups, learners compose their own religious songs, sing in class and discuss the key message. [cite: 8167]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.2.1: Analyse and apply the moral values in religious songs and recitations
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of basic scripture recitations/oral traditions in the three major religions
examplars: 1. Recite basic texts from the Bible, Qur'an and Oral traditions. Bible (Psalm 23, Exodus 20), Qur'an (Al-Fatihah, Al-Ikhlas), Oral tradition (Proverbs). [cite: 8172]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 2.2.1: Analyse and apply the moral values in religious songs and recitations
indicator_description: Identify the moral values in religious songs and recitations.
examplars:
1. Discuss the moral values in religious songs and recitations. E.g. motivation, humility, contentment, acknowledgement of God's power. [cite: 8172]
2. With the help of role play, learners demonstrate how they apply the moral values in their daily life. [cite: 8172]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 3.1.1: Identify and Explain the Importance of the Family Systems
indicator_description: Explain the concept and types of family systems in Ghana
examplars:
1. Learners explain the concept of family. [cite: 8176]
2. Learners identify the types of family systems in Ghana (Nuclear and Extended). [cite: 8176]
3. Describe the two-family systems in Ghana. [cite: 8176]
4. With the help of parents, learners create/draw a poster to show their family tree. [cite: 8176]
5. Discuss the merits and demerits of both family systems. [cite: 8176]
6. Project Work: Debate on the motion "The extended family system is more important than the nuclear family system." [cite: 8181]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 3.1.1: Identify and Explain the Importance of the Family Systems
indicator_description: Identify the role of family members.
examplars: 1. In a discussion, learners identify the roles of children, parents and grandparents in the family. [cite: 8181]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 3.1.1: Identify and Explain the Importance of the Family Systems
indicator_description: Describe ways of promoting good relationships among family members
examplars:
1. Identify factors that promote good relationships. [cite: 8186]
2. Identify the need for healthy relationships. [cite: 8186]
3. Analyse the need for self-examination when things go wrong. [cite: 8186]
4. Discuss the importance of family systems in Ghana. [cite: 8186]
5. Dramatise behaviours that show good relationships among family members. [cite: 8186]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 4.1.1: Identify the key features and moral messages of the call and ministry of Religious Leaders in the three major religions.
indicator_description: Discuss the early life and call of the religious leaders
examplars:
1. Show pictures or video clips to depict birth and early life (Jesus Christ, Muhammad (SAW), Okomfo Anokye, etc.). [cite: 8191]
2. Summarise the birth and early life of religious leaders. [cite: 8191]
3. In groups, discuss the call of the leaders (Baptism/Temptation of Jesus, Muhammad at Makkah, etc.). [cite: 8191]
4. Role play the call of the religious leaders and show moral lessons. [cite: 8191]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 4.1.1: Identify the key features and moral messages of the call and ministry of Religious Leaders in the three major religions.
indicator_description: Describe the ministries of the religious leaders
examplars:
1. Summarise the key events associated with the ministry (Jesus' Sermon on the Mount, Muhammad's entry to Makkah, etc.). [cite: 8198]
2. Project Work: In three groups, pick one leader and create a poster indicating key life events. [cite: 8198]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 4.1.1: Identify the key features and moral messages of the call and ministry of Religious Leaders in the three major religions.
indicator_description: Discuss the moral lessons from the exemplary lives of the religious leaders.
examplars:
1. Identify the moral lessons (humility, forgiveness, etc.). [cite: 8198]
2. Role play virtues and demonstrate how to apply them. [cite: 8198]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.1.1: Develop good manners and learn how to apply them in their daily lives.
indicator_description: Identify and explain behaviours considered to be good manners in the society.
examplars: 1. Explain the meaning of "manners" and "decency." [cite: 8201]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.1.1: Develop good manners and learn how to apply them in their daily lives.
indicator_description: Discuss the importance of good manners and decency
examplars: 1. Pick examples for discussion (Greetings, Decent dressing). Discuss factors to consider and the need for each. [cite: 8201]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.1.1: Develop good manners and learn how to apply them in their daily lives.
indicator_description: Discuss the significance of chastity
examplars:
1. Explain the term "chastity". [cite: 8207]
2. Indicate ways of leading a chaste life. [cite: 8207]
3. State the importance of living a chaste life. [cite: 8207]
4. Role play ways of leading a chaste life. [cite: 8207]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.1.1: Develop good manners and learn how to apply them in their daily lives.
indicator_description: Discuss the teachings of the three main religions on manners, decency and chastity
examplars: 1. Identify and explain examples (Indigenous African Religion culture, Christianity Bible verses, Islam Qur'an verses). [cite: 8233]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.2.1: Demonstrate the need to stay away from immoral thoughts and actions, with a key focus on substance abuse
indicator_description: Summarise the causes of substance abuse
examplars:
1. Brainstorm on meaning. [cite: 8239]
2. Identify types of substances abused (Common, Hard, Stimulants). [cite: 8239]
3. Discuss causes (Ignorance, peer pressure, curiosity). [cite: 8239]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.2.1: Demonstrate the need to stay away from immoral thoughts and actions, with a key focus on substance abuse
indicator_description: Discuss the effects of substance abuse.
examplars:
1. Show pictures/videos of effects. [cite: 8246]
2. Identify and explain effects (Abnormal behaviour, ill-health, economic problems, etc.). [cite: 8246]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 5.2.1: Demonstrate the need to stay away from immoral thoughts and actions, with a key focus on substance abuse
indicator_description: Demonstrate knowledge of teachings from the three religions on defensive skills in protecting yourself against substance abuse
examplars:
1. Identify and explain teachings (Christianity, Islam, Indigenous). [cite: 8253]
2. Moral Lessons (Adherence to teachings, Say no, Seek help, etc.). [cite: 8253]
3. Dramatise effects and application of lessons. [cite: 8253]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 6.1.1: Cultivate the need for hard work and develop the spirit of entrepreneurship
indicator_description: Explain the meaning of work and entrepreneurship
examplars:
1. Distinguish between concepts (work, entrepreneurship, social security). [cite: 8258]
2. Identify and explain types of work (Domestic, Community, School, Livelihood, Religious). [cite: 8258]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 6.1.1: Cultivate the need for hard work and develop the spirit of entrepreneurship
indicator_description: Discuss the relevance of work from the religious and ethical perspectives
examplars:
1. Demonstrate knowledge of good work habits (punctuality, hard work, advance planning, dedication). [cite: 8264]
2. Demonstrate the need to work (Importance: basic needs, health, talent development). [cite: 8264]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 6.1.1: Cultivate the need for hard work and develop the spirit of entrepreneurship
indicator_description: Identify steps to be taken to become a successful entrepreneur
examplars: 1. State steps (goal setting, innovation, risk taking, self-belief, marketing, capital, human resource). [cite: 8267]

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B7/JHS1 6.1.1: Cultivate the need for hard work and develop the spirit of entrepreneurship
indicator_description: Justify the need to become an entrepreneur
examplars:
1. Discuss benefits (freedom of vision, flexibility, employment, nation-building, pride). [cite: 8275]
2. Project Work: Field research in the community into how to become successful and present findings. [cite: 8282]
